prompt: The Letterpress package renders bundled text templates correctly from the source checkout, and its source-tree tests pass. In the release job, however, a freshly built wheel installs successfully but render_welcome() raises FileNotFoundError because the templates are missing from the installed artifact. Inspect the package and its build metadata, then make the smallest production-only repair so wheels contain the package templates. Preserve the public API and rendered output, do not add runtime fallbacks or generated copies, and keep editable installs working. Run the hermetic acceptance command below; it exercises source-tree behavior, wheel contents, a wheel-only install, and an editable install. Do not modify the tests.
verify_cmd: env PIP_NO_INDEX=1 PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -v

full_distill.primary_skill: inspect Python build metadata, distinguish source-checkout behavior from built-distribution behavior, and verify package data through isolated wheel and editable installations
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the source-tree tests and editable-install test pass, but the acceptance command exits 1 because letterpress/templates/welcome.txt is absent from the wheel and render_welcome raises FileNotFoundError from a wheel-only installation
full_distill.success_contract: a freshly built wheel contains the declared template and renders the exact welcome text after isolated installation, while source-tree behavior and an actual editable installation continue to work
adversarial_factors: the ordinary tests import from src and pass on the broken baseline, so source-checkout success cannot stand in for distribution validation
the protected suite inspects the wheel archive and separately invokes the package from a pip-installed target under isolated Python, rejecting fixes that accidentally import the checkout
an actual PEP 660 editable installation must still locate and render the source template, rejecting metadata repairs that only make the wheel path work by breaking development installs
all builds and installs run in fresh temporary copies with PIP_NO_INDEX and no build isolation, so cached artifacts and network downloads cannot hide the packaging defect
